title: Occult Complications
description: Scene-level complications that emerge during occult operations — the things that go sideways when you work in spaces where the rules are different.

entries.result: The ward is older than the building. It was designed for a different purpose and a different location and was moved here at some point. It may not work as expected.
The entity the crew is dealing with has already made a deal with someone else in the room, prior to this meeting.
The object is not the object. It's a very good copy made by someone who had access to the original for long enough to understand it.
The target location is already occupied by another party performing a different operation that is interfering with this one.
The binding that was supposed to contain the relevant entity was dissolved three days ago. The space looks the same. The entity is not in it.
The ritual requires a component that the crew has, but using it will exhaust it. The component is also needed for something else.
The contact who was supposed to be reliable turns out to have been compromised. Their information was accurate — but filtered.
The door opens to a different space than expected — correct building, correct time, different floor or room.
A witness. Not hostile, not allied. Simply present, and now aware.
The object is protected by something that is not a ward — it's a relationship. Whoever takes it takes on a relationship they don't know they're in.
The entity the crew is negotiating with is not the entity they believe it to be. Same category. Different individual. Different terms.
Something has already taken what the crew was sent to retrieve, recently, from the traces.
The client's information was accurate when they gave it. It stopped being accurate sometime yesterday.
A working that was supposed to be inert activates in response to a crew member's presence. The crew member is not sure why.
The job was meant to fail. The crew was meant to be present for its failure. The reason is not immediately clear.
Something was left behind during the previous operation in this location — not by the crew, but by someone using the same location.
The neutralizing agent works but also neutralizes something else that was doing useful work without the crew's awareness.
What seemed like one binding is two, nested. The outer one has been handled. The inner one is a different problem.
A crew member begins perceiving something the others can't. The perception is accurate. It is also distracting at a critical moment.
The operation succeeds. The consequences arrive ahead of schedule.
The target knows the crew is coming. They have left something for them instead of defending the location.
A third party has been monitoring the location and records the crew's presence before they complete the operation.
The entity agrees to the terms offered. This turns out to be unexpected, because no one should have agreed to these terms.
A component of the working is not where it's supposed to be. It was moved by someone performing routine maintenance with no awareness of its significance.
The operation leaves a trace that is distinctive and recognizable to anyone who knows how to look.
The location is already claimed. The prior claim predates the current tenant by a century and it has not been discharged.
An alarm exists that the crew didn't know about because it reports not to a person but to something that does not notify in ways the crew would recognize.
The target object is heavier than its size suggests, in ways that are not physical.
The crew succeeds but is seen by someone who shouldn't have been present and now knows something they can use.
What the client wanted retrieved is inside what the client wanted left behind. The instructions are technically contradictory.
